name: SYN Community Spin
description:
Get your bike ready for a season of indoor riding with the SYN Cycling Club crew. Every Monday we ride a 90-minutes group ride on various maps, from city circuits to mountainuous loops. This group ride is C-categorized, meaning our ride leader leads the pack at a pace of 2.5-3.2 w/kg. Finish one group ride and you unlock the exclusive SYN in-game club kit. Meet up with us for some great stories and ask us any of your questions.

UNLOCK THE SYNDICATE CLUB KIT
Finish a SYN Community Spin on zwift and unlock the SYN club kit in your garage, so you can wear the colours of our cycling club in any other ride on Zwift.

DISCOVER MORE IN THE SYN ZWIFT CLUB
Enjoyed this ride? Feel free to join our Zwift Club (SYN Cycling Club), and discover all our events:
https://bit.ly/SYNZwiftClub

name: Team DRAFT Monday Race
description:
Welcome to the Monday Race brought to you by Team DRAFT!
We encourage that all participants head over to zwiftpower.com and register. For more information see: https://zwiftinsider.com/zwiftpower-signup/ Riders who have not completed the Zwiftpower registration will not be included in the official race results. We also ask that your Zwift activities be saved publicly, and that you open and maintain a Strava account to allow for organizer analysis. Any rider exhibiting questionable performance during a the race may be disqualified.

RULES:
- No zPower riders.
- Heart rate monitors mandatory for all categories
- 5min and 20min CEVAZ limits apply. Riding in the wrong category will lead to disqualification.

RACE CATEGORIES:
A: 4.0 W/kg to 5.0 W/kg
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg
D: Under 2.49 W/kg

name: Ride Club Finland: Category Spotlight
description:
Welcome to weekly Category Spotlight races brought to you by Ride Club Finland.

You'll see only riders from your own pace category:

A/A+ experiences pure elite racing without the safety net of (s)lower category riders. Every move matters when you know that all other riders are capable of responding to your attacks.

B gets authentic racing without A riders disappearing into the horizon and the temptation to follow at an unsustainable pace. Instead, you must learn your true competitors' strengths and weaknesses to succeed.

C builds confidence without much stronger riders messing up the race dynamics. In these races, you initiate attacks and control race situations until the winner is decided by crossing the finish line.

D experiences the full spectrum of racing - from early attacks to final sprints. The winner is determined by pure racing ability against equally matched competitors, not by drafting behind stronger category riders for the longest.

Your category. Your chance.

name: Kind Monday ride rubber band with Bikes world
description:
If you have been ill, mistreated by life, wounded or if you just want gently starting the week, this ride is for you. Join Bikes world’s family to share a good time together.
Le ride bienveillant en mode élastique du lundi de Bikes world.
Si vous avez été malade, maltraité par la vie, blessé ou si vous avez envie de commencer doucement la semaine, ce ride est pour vous.
Rejoignez la famille Bikes World afin de passer un bon moment ensemble.
Ce sera en mode élastique.

name: Cycle Nation Joburg Sundowner
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Joburg Sundowner Ride. Join us for an easy evening social ride.

Your co-operation is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the ride leader (yellow beacon).

This is not a race and we encourage you to stay with the ride leader (yellow beacon) and if you are feeling strong you are more than welcome to assist with sweeping duties at the back of the ride helping dropped riders rejoin the group. We're looking forward to hosting you for the a ride.

Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Twitter: https://bit.ly/3n4uhft
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: Women's Workout with Rocacorba Collective
description:
Join us for a Women's Only Workout Ride.

This workout is designed to be inclusive and can be ridden however you choose!
It can be treated as a Workout, Recovery Ride, Pre-Race activation or just join us for some social chats .. either way, we are banded and we all stick together.

Session: Micro Sprints
We will Warm Up for 10 mins, followed by 5 Mirco Burst Sprints (20secs) and finish with a 10 mins Cool Down

What is the Rocacorba Collective?
Rocacorba Collective is a membership-based indoor cycling community. It was created by Ashleigh Moolman Pasio in 2021 with the mission to revolutionise the indoor e-cycling experience by creating an inspiring, uplifting and safe space for women to come together to cycle, whatever their level or experience.

Route: We will ride the route for the upcoming race during WTRL seasons and explore other routes in the off-season.

Sign up here : www.rocacorbacollective.cc
Meet our Ambassadors : https://www.rocacorbacycling.cc/meet-your-ds

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Vive in Nisu [VIN] - Motion Monday
description:
Join the Vive In Nisu group ride.

This ride is not a race. Please help the Sweepers out if you‘ve some extra energy left.

Goal is to stay with the yellow beacon around the advertised pace for maximum draft.

Let‘s enjoy the effort together and focus on positive thoughts and great company☀️

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: EVO CC Vamos! [3.5-4w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Vamos! The ride is geared
towards Zwifter’s that are looking for a harder than normal session. The
average for the ride will be between 3.5-4w/kg depending on riders
weight, course and leader. The ride will include intervals above and/or
below the advertised figures as they are only an average.


There will be no keepers for this ride - if dropped, please form a 2nd
group.

The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter.
Please use the companion app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

name: Rebellunas Banded Ride
description:
Starte deinen Montag mit den Rebellunas. Ein Social Ride, bei dem das Peloton, dank Rubber Band, zusammenbleibt. Gemeinsam sammeln wir Kilometer, Höhenmeter und jagen Streckenabzeichen, alles in einem entspannten Tempo von 1,6 bis 2 W/kg. Lehn dich zurück, genieße den Ride und entdecke, wie viel Spaß Teamgeist macht. Bist du dabei?

Kick off your Monday with the Rebellunas! A Social Ride where the peloton stays together, thanks to the Rubber Band. Together we’ll collect kilometers, climb meters, and chase route badges – all at a relaxed pace of 1.6 to 2 W/kg. Sit back, enjoy the ride, and discover how much fun team spirit can be. Are you in?

name: ZZRC Wrecked Legs - Post Weekend Recovery Ride
description:
Welcome to our  ZZRC Wrecked Legs 2.5-2.7wkg. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together. 

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2.5-2.7 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints  , but the average for the whole ride is as advertised

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced . 

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch . 

This js a Cat C medium paced group ride . It is not a slow paced Cat D Ride . 

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you . 

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group . 

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON . 

Sweepers are not in operation on this ride so please consider using late join in the first 30 mins or make a small group of your own. 

We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord 
  https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX

TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone 

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

eventSubgroups.name: ZZRC Wrecked Legs - Post Weekend Recovery Ride (C)

name: NGNM UNITED WE ARE Women's Workout Series
description:
Join our United We Are 5-Month Women’s Training Plan — a progressive program to get you ready for any real-life cycling challenge, from NGNM's United We Are Tour to gravel events, granfondos, or your next big goal.

You’ll build endurance, strength, and climbing power through 14 weeks of structured base work + 5 weeks of focused climbing training.

Train with **Mim Taylor - 2025 UK National Gravel Champion & UCI Worlds multi-podium athlete -** and Milly De Mori, NGNM’s founder.
You show up. We guide the way.
Because training solo is tough — but with United We Are, everything gets easier. 💪

⚡️🚴‍♀️ Event Details:

Workouts: < 1 hour

Community: We stay together—whatever your watts or FTP

Chat: Join us on Discord https://discord.gg/wywQsUaRYX
 or Zwift Companion's chat for support & motivation

💥 BONUS: Unlock NGNM’s exclusive WRL Kit on Zwift!

👉 Join NGNM’s United We Are Cycling Club on Companion:
https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/19908def-e39c-466b-9737-4db8bb40e503/join

—————
About No Gods No Masters® (NGNM) — Smart Cycling Apparel for Women
NGNM is more than a cycling brand: it’s a movement empowering women through style, performance, and freedom on&off the bike. Alongside high-end apparel, NGNM runs United We Are—exclusive road and gravel events across Italy and Europe, designed to bring women riders together on unforgettable adventures.
https://nogodsnomasters.life/pages/united-we-are-events

Order the RL of the NGNM jersey on Zwift here:
https://nogodsnomasters.life/collections/ngnm-x-zwift

name: Mountain Massif structured training: Aerobic Stretch 1 (70-80%)
description:
Created by Matt Rowe, from Rowe & King Coaching, this structured training session is
designed to help you cycle at a higher intensity. 
For coaching and all your training needs visit https://roweandking.com/
About the session:
To start with there is a progressive warmup, followed by 3 blocks of ramped efforts - 70-80%
of FTP across 12 minutes. The first two steps ramp up, whilst the final ramp is regressive
and ramps down. There are 4-minutes recovery, between blocks. A warm down completes
the workout.
Safety Note:
Only undertake vigorous exercise if you are fit and healthy to do so. If you are at all unsure,
consult a Physician or Doctor first. All training equipment including Bikes and Turbo trainers
should be in full working order. Do not use any piece of equipment that does not come up to
the required standard.
More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow
on Facebook, Instagram (Mountain_Massif) and join our Zwift Club by clicking the following
link https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join. To
record all your training data and chart your progress, consider using the TrainingPeaks app
(www.trainingpeaks.com/).

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Southern Coast Cruise
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: KIRCHMAIR Honeypot Ride
description:
🇩🇪Willkommen zum KIRCHMAIR Honeypot Ride. Jeden Montag veranstalten wir eine Gruppenfahrt mit lockerem Tempo (~2 w/kg) in einer gemeinsamen Gruppe. Wenn ihr in der Gruppe nahe unseres Leaders mit dem gelben Icon bleibt, profitiert ihr von doppeltem Windschatten!
Viel Spaß ☺️

🇺🇸 Welcome to the KIRCHMAIR Honeypot Ride. Every Monday we‘re hosting a group ride with an easy pace (~2 w/kg). Stay close to the yellow leader beacon in the main group to take advantage of double draft!
Have fun ☺️

name: TNP 100km Upgrade ride
description:
This is not a race. This is a choice.							
Ride alone… or ride together.							
We’re covering 100km on the Cadex Tri (disc wheel, draft enabled) to stack serious kilometres towards bike upgrades.							
"👉 Go solo and ride at your own pace
👉 Or work as a group and share the load
👉 Sit in, take turns, or test yourself off the front

There’s no single way to do this. The only goal is simple:

Get the kilometres done.

💡 WHAT TO EXPECT
• Groups will naturally form
• Some will push the pace, others will settle in
• You might get dropped… or you might ride away

🔧 WHY THIS RIDE?
• Fast-track bike upgrades
• Build endurance over a full 100km
• Ride it your way. No pressure, no tactics

👊 RULES
• Cadex Tri bike only (disc wheel) doube draft enabled
• No need to race, no need to wait
• Ride your effort, your plan

Whether you sit in a group or take it on solo…
every kilometre counts."

name: REVO Slow Roll SUB1.5
description:
Join the women of REVO for a 1-1.5 w/kg paced ride for 30 minutes on the flattest courses we can find. The route will change week to week, but the concept will not. This ride is geared towards those wanting a slower paced ride than REVO's other offerings, whether this is your ""all out"" pace, a recovery ride, or part of your plan for injury recovery or staying fit during your pregnancy. All genders welcome!

Please respect the pace of the yellow beacon and stay close by. If you are feeling strong, please consider volunteering as a sweeper to pull people back onto the pack. Let the group know if you're available to help!

#revo

name: Ride 4 all levels
description:
JOIN OUR NEW CLUB for non-french riders on Companion : club BIKES WORLD,

English spoken !

The idea is to bring together riders from all over the world, no matter where you come from, no matter your level, no matter if you are already in a club or not; just have fun !

A new route every Monday! A great idea to make sure you complete as many routes as possible and earn points.

Everyone rides at their own pace.

Open to everyone from D to A.

***FRENCH***
Soyez les bienvenu(e)s dans le ride du club BIKES (tag : BIKES)

Un nouveau parcours tous les lundis ! une bonne idée pour être certain de terminer un maximum de parcours et gagner des points.

Ouvert à tous du D au A

Rythme : pas de rythme imposé, chacun roule à son rythme,

Rejoignez-nous ! ajoute le tag BIKES à ton pseudo Zwift et rejoins le club BIKES FRANCE

sur Companion

Discutez sur Discord: https://discord.gg/a8wYvgw

Notre Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikes

Rejoins-nous : https://www.bikesfrance.com/

eventSubgroups.name: Ride 4 all levels (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
FRENCH :
Soyez les bienvenu(e)s à la sortie de groupe de la Team BIKES France (tag : BIKES-FR)
« Roulez seul c’est bien mais rouler en groupe et entre amis c’est encore mieux ! »
Le but de cette sortie est de pédaler tous ensemble dans la bonne humeur durant une petite heure.
L’itinéraire est roulant, pour cette sortie on évite les grosses montées, c’est donc ouvert à tout le monde, no stress.

Règle de bonne conduite :
On roule en peloton = ce n’est pas une course !
Rythme moyen global max 2,5 w/kg
Merci de rester autour de la balise jaune du leader

Discutez sur Discord avec nous pendant la sortie : https://discord.gg/a8wYvgw
Découvrez-nous sur Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikesFrance
Rejoignez officiellement la team BIKES-FR et profiter d’entrainements et de sorties privés et/ou des courses: https://www.bikes-france.com/index.php/zwift/devenir-bikes-fr
ENGLISH :
Welcome to the Team BIKES France group outing (tag: BIKES-FR)
"Riding alone is good, but riding in a group and with friends is even better! "
The goal of this outing is to pedal all together in a good mood for an hour.
The route is rolling, for this outing we avoid big climbs, so it is open to everyone, no stress.

Rule of good conduct:
We ride in a peloton = it's not a race!
Overall average rhythm max 2.5 w/kg
Please stay around the yellow leader's tag

Chat on Discord with us during the release: https://discord.gg/a8wYvgw
Discover us on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftBikesFrance
Official website : https://bikes-france.com

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: GRU PETA-Z
description:
It's PAC-MAN time!

Join the PETA-Z team in this thrilling event! You have two pace options: C (2.5-2.8 w/kg) or D (1.9-2.3 w/kg). The groups will start 5 minutes apart, with a clear target: to hunt or to avoid being hunted!

Respect the pace of the leaders and work as a team! Who will show the best collaboration? Who will save energy for the final sprint?

Get the best-looking and most original jersey on Zwift by completing this route.

Chat with us on Zwift or join the conversation on Discord.
Our website: http://www.peta-zeta.com
Join Team PETA-Z on https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=10683
Live broadcast on Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/petazetateam
#kitunlock #petazetateam

name: Zwift Epic Race - The Muckle Yin
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

name: ZLDR Monday Chill Hour Run
description:
ZLDR Monday Chill Hour Run
 

Not motivated because it’s Monday and this day is the start of a new busy week ?

Come join us for one hour of fun in the ZLDR Monday Chill Hour Run !

No pressure about racing here !

It’s all about cooling down from the weekend and sharing positive vibes for this new week ahead !

We wait you !

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!


For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020


Strava Club: 
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020

https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/

https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

name: 3R Impact Interval Ride [~3.7-4.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ 

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - The Fan Flats
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: DraftingDinos Monday FunDay
description:
This ride is for D riders looking for a nice route recon or steady pace ride. The focus for this group ride is to have fun and get to know the various routes within Zwift. During ZRL season we will be riding the course that corresponds with that week’s race. The pace will be between 1.5wkg-1.9wkg throughout the duration of the route.

ABOUT THIS SERIES OF EVENTS
This ride is lead by DraftingDinos race team. We have riders of all abilities and field race teams for women’s only and Mixed Gender races. From riders new to cycling, to those who have experience racing we want to make your experience fun! If you’re interested in checking out our race team please see our Facebook page Draftingdinos race team on Zwift.

name: ESRT group WO
description:
Welcome to join team eSRT for a group workout.
When the days are getting shorter and the air is getting cooler, that can only mean one thing. It’s time for Zwift racing to heat up! We’ve collected some structured workouts that can help you get more ready for racing. Try them out together with us to boost your fitness and mental strength. Then enjoy racing with your newfound gains!

name: Monday Run Club
description:
Start your week off right with a group run hosted by the Zwift Runners Facebook Group. We’ll ease into another week of training alongside the community while actively recovering from any long runs you might have had during the weekend. With runs in seven time slots with four pace groups each (A being the fastest and D being the slowest), there’s an event for everyone. 

Pace Groups
A) 7.5–8.5 mph // 12–14 kph
B) 7–7.5 mph // 10.5–12 kph
C) 6–6.5 mph // 9.5–10.5 kph
D) 5–5.5 mph // 8–9 kph

We’ll be using workout mode throughout these runs so everyone can easily determine their speed. Though these runs utilize a mostly steady pace, there will be some speed changes for variety.

If you have any questions about this event or want to find out more about Zwift Run in general, then please visit the Zwift Runners Facebook Group or zwift.com/run

#gozwiftrun #mrc

name: Rowe & King - Monday Mixer
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

Rowe & King's Monday Mixer is a short but focused session, full of structure. In terms of physical stress - the session is regarded as "moderate" - one that can be completed following a hard Sunday ride, and should not leave your legs too fatigued come Tuesday. This session is regarded as 'High Intensity Interval Training' (HIIT) and fits in perfectly alongside longer endurance rides on the weekend.

In terms of physical stress - the session is regarded as "moderate" - one that can be completed following a hard Sunday ride, and should not leave your legs too fatigued come Tuesday. 

This session is regarded as 'High Intensity Interval Trainng' (HIIT) and fits in perfectly alongside longer endurance rides on the weekend. 

WORKOUT DESIGNER

This session is designed by Rowe & King Head Coach, Courtney Rowe who has been coaching cyclists for over 30 years from beginner to professionals, and is a firm believer in quality over quantity. 

Courtney is 29 x Welsh Champion, 5 x British Champion, 4 x European Champion and has 5 World Championship medals in his trophy cabinet! 

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins.  Everyone stays together regardless of power output.   That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other.  Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.   

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: SZR Evening Joyride
description:
Welcome to the SZR (Swedish Zwift Riders) and the Evening Joyride which is the oldest SZR Event, and it started in September 2015 for the first time.
This ride is a ride for all categories of riders, that is why we have a lot of categories to choose from in this ride so make sure to pick a category that fits your needs.

- The goal with the SZR group ride is to focus on the group and keeping the group together the entire ride.
- Make sure to choose the correct group. We split up in four groups B, C, D and E group.
Group B -> 3.2 - 4.0 w/kg
Group C -> 2.5 - 3.0 w/kg
Group D -> 2.1 - 2,4 w/kg
Group E -> 1.5 - 2.0 w/kg

- This is a social group ride and not a race. Staying close to the ride leader is the key.
- Please follow the ride leaders instructions at all times.
- After steep hills we focus on keeping the group together and soft pedal to let the group come together again.
Use our discord if you like to have a chat with the people on the ride
https://discord.gg/DQHybQT

#szr

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Zwift Beginners Weekly Group Workouts
description: Zwift Beginners is a welcoming place for all Zwift and cycling newbies looking to get in a group workout and find community! No pace is too slow, all questions are welcome and Ride Ons are given freely! These workouts will run Mondays and Wednesdays every 3 hours and be no more than 30 minutes (short and sweet). They are designed to give you a taste of what is possible on Zwift, nothing too hard or intimidating. All workouts are banded which means no matter what your level, we will all stick together! See you soon! Join the Zwift Beginner Club here https://www.zwift.com/.../b355516b-0136-45f8-ae6e.../join

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Beginners Weekly Group Workouts (E)
eventSubgroups.description: Powerful and efficient, this high-intensity interval workout uses short bursts mixed with recovery periods to build up your energy and strength. In classic HIIT form, you'll do seven sets of 30 seconds of hard effort, and 1 minute of rest in between. Lucky you.

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Rocacorba Collective)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Rocacorba Collective (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/322091e4-9052-4f4e-9e65-f90bf0bb6e99

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Zwift Turkiye - TREK RMK
description:
This ride was created in Turkey to ensure that bicycle lovers around the world come together and socialize on Zwift. We, as the Zwift Turkey team, have had enjoyed this ride in our club for a long time, but we want to reach more people and make cycling training more enjoyable together on a regular basis. We'd love to have you join us! 

The aim of our event is to enable cyclists who train intensively on the weekend to rest and socialize at the same time. To enable our high-energy friends to climb the volcano at the end of the tour and release their energy.

name: PAOLO BARBERA ROLLING
description:
Please visit www.heycoach.it or contact me
Coach Paolo Barbera is a Certified Triathlon and Ironman Coach and the founder
of Active Kids, a sporting clinic to help children and adults in the prevention
and treatment of obesity through healthy nutrition and tailored training
programmes.
Paolo is an experienced triathlete and cyclist. In 2017 he opened an innovative
endurance training centre in milan, Tri60, specialized in indoor cycling.
It has been proven that Indoor cycling plays a key role to build strength,
resistance and improve overall fitness and technical skills.
Paolo’s workouts are designed to mix different training zones aiming to maximize
results in a very short time. Athletes following his workouts, at the right
intensity and at the right cadence, have achieved surprising results.
Paolo’s indoor cycling workouts have different lengths to meet every athlete
needs.
In the last ten years Paolo has developed very successful training programs for
triathletes and cyclists
IT IS NOT HOW YOU START BUT HOW YOU FINISH!
GROUP WORKOUTS
Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normaL event and once the
event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power
output. We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests to face every workout at
the right difficulty level.

name: PUMP: Loop Logic I — Monday (1 Lap)
description:
Same Distance. More Laps. Maximum Sass. ♥️🌶️

Welcome to Loop Logic, the weekday series where we prove that "going in circles" is actually a superpower. We’ve curated a week of routes that hit the 19–25km sweet spot with enough climbing to keep your glutes talking back to you.

The Logic is Simple:
We start with one big loop on Monday and add a lap every day until Friday. One lap is a warm-up, but five laps is a statement.

The PUMP Vibe:
Power in Numbers: No band here, ladies! This is your chance to find your rhythm, tuck into a friendly draft, and realize you’re much faster than you think.

Elevation is Our Love Language: Expect at least 100m of climb—just enough to feel the PUMP and earn that post-ride coffee.

Laughter > Watts: We ride hard, but we laugh harder. Whether you’re smashing a PR or just hanging onto a wheel for dear life, do it with a smile.

The Mission:
Show up for yourself, stay for the sisterhood, and let’s show exactly what women-powered logic looks like.

name: Le Col Social Recovery Sessions
description:
Welcome to the weekly Le Col Social Recovery Sessions. Led by ex-professional cyclist, Chris Opie.

We only become stronger when we recover. Making time for recovery sessions is often overlooked, at Le Col we want to offer you training sessions to help you improve your on the bike fitness. Train with us every weekday, build towards our challenges and improve your fitness with our coach led sessions.

Our training sessions are a great opportunity to ask our resident experts, our guest riders and our ambassadors anything you like.
Want to know how to get more out of your cycling, nutrition or simply ask our ambassadors about their race days? This is the ride for you!
Train towards challenges, QOMs, KOMs, distance goals, participation goals and build your Zwift XP in good company.

Submit your favourite motivational songs to our collaborative Spotify playlist.
https://open.spotify.com/playlist/7gngntJJnJZ7La3KjCOhR4
For mor information on our training sessions, head here.
https://lecol.cc/pages/events?series=train-with-pros

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: DBR Wahoo Spring Cup
description:
Welcome to DBR Wahoo Spring Cup.

5 races where we guarantee sweat and a high heart rate rate . All 5 races will count and the riders with most points will win the General Classification. 
Results and Standings can be seen on zwiftpower.com

All will start with 1 minute between RS categories. Expect each race to be highly competitive and have fun.
Categories. If you move up a RS group during the Cup, the points you got, will stay in the group you got them. 

Each race will be using Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) for categorization.
race up a category, if your RS is at the top end of a category. Or if you simply desire an added challenge.

- A: 1000 =< 690
- B:690<520
- C:520<350
- D:350<180
- E:180<=0

Your Zwift Racing Score is determined by how much success you have in races.
better against people with higher ZRS, then your ZRS may increase. If you place worse than people with lower ZRS, then your score might decrease. Read more about ZRS here - https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh <https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh>
If your ZRS increases past a category boundary during the series, your points earned will not transfer to the new category with you.


Races:
1:  Watts the Limit - April 13
2: London Loop - April 20
3: InnsbruckRing - April 27
4: Makuri 40 - May 4
5: Itza Climb Finish - May 11
 

RULES:
Smart trainer or power meter is required. zPower will be removed.
Heart rate monitors are compulsory for all categories.
No TT bikes unless it's a TT event.
You need to have a registered account with Zwiftpower.com and accept their terms and conditions to be in the results of the race.
Misc.
Race organizers may request riders in all categories to provide a dual recording or a matching RL effort (Strava) in order to verify a Zwift race effort. Failure to comply may result in disqualification from the event. Failure to provide a dual recording or RL effort on three requests may result in disqualification from future events, until a dual recording with a matching effort is provided.
Race organizers may exclude a rider from the results, due to poor sportsmanship. Examples may be bad language, sandbagging or other behavior of a disruptive manner.

#danishbikeriders #danishbikerider #wahoo #Wahoonordics

name: ZZRC Spin Out Sub 2
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Spin Out Sub 2. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.7-1.9 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be sub 2 .

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel for our sub 2
https://discord.gg/RJ3dhbjCEp


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Southern Coast Cruise
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to GXY ORBIT the ride with a twin space shuttle workout theme!
A 60 minute journey where the yellow and red beacons fly together as space shuttles bringing a workout that includes
blast offs, thrusts and power shifts whilst keeping all the riders in one aerodynamic blob. .
What is for certain, is that this ride has a completely different style and vibe and brings a fun atmosphere whilst we
work together
Please take a break from the norm and join us with our ORBIT ride, it's guaranteed to bring a smile to your face with
jokes, friendly banter whilst undertaking an achievable zonal workout at the same time to achieve your fitness goals.
GXY are a friendly inclusive and supportive club who strive to help others succeed. We are looking forward to riding
with you today.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link below:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: ZHCC RUBBERBAND CLUB
description:
Join Lee ZHCC Founder and Head Honch to start the week with a 100% guaranteed no drop ride (unless you stop pedalling) meaning you stay with the group riding at your own pace? If you want the experience of a group ride with no drop and no nasty fence then this might be just what you are looking for. Our ZHCC Rubber Band Club has the “rubber-band” activated which means that no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. The rubber band allows you to ride at your own pace, slow or fast and you are guaranteed to stay in the group.

In this supportive environment it’s a great way of completing a full 1-hour ride knowing you won’t get dropped or get dragged along by those naughty fliers!
ZHCC do not race each other, we ride as one, have fun and enjoy a group ride.
The ZHCC gang look forward to riding with you.
STRAVA : https://www.strava.com/clubs/518792
ZHCC Facebook page - https://www.facebook.com/groups/1163022533747527/
DISCORD : https://www.discord.gg/ukSdEQd

name: ZRG-CC - Afterwork Ride
description:
Kick-in your week with a medium paced endurance ride hosted by ZRG Cycling Club. Aimed at building endurance, giving an attained workout and curing hangovers from last weekend. Our group ride event is designed with an intensity range between 2.6-3.2 W/kg but may include short intervals well over and below the ride average parameters pushing you to the sky. 

Teamwork is essential, we aim to support each other. Please stay together as a group around the Yellow Beacon at all times. We don’t chase flyers, it’s not a race. Fence will be available, only varied to allow for hill climbs or planned sprints with immediate regrouping afterwards. Sweepers are not in operation. Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help sweep the riders struggling back to the pack. Be sure to listen to the ride leader’s instructions.

Nobody likes being dropped. But things get stretched and this is not a “no drop ride”. If you’re fall of the back of the pack missing the draft effect means you are lost. You need to pace significantly over main group pace to bridge the gap and catch. Otherwise check the mini-map for riders around you, form a gruppetto with them and support each other.

You can find more information about upcoming events in our Facebook community (https://www.facebook.com/ZwiftRidersGermany/) and we welcome any feedback. If you get through the event you will unlock the ZwiftRidersGermany Jersey. Late joining is available. 

Join us for as long as you want, enjoy your training and make new Zwift buddies. 
Have a good time. #FunIsFast

--
Find us on strava at https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-riders-germany
Please, join us on Discord at https://discord.gg/tv567Pv
And, Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/ZwiftRidersGermany/

#zrg

name: EVOLVE 15km Road Race
description:
EVENT DESCRIPTION
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 15km Road Race, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply
staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: Zwift Epic Race - The Muckle Yin
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: Bikealicious Zone 2 Fat Burner Ride
description: This is a rubberband Zone 2 fat-burning ride. While the primary goal is to maintain a Zone 2 intensity, feel free to ride at any pace you prefer. We have riders of all levels; all are welcome. We support less experienced riders and draw inspiration from those with more strength and expertise. This is a social ride, so don't be afraid to jump in and chat

name: ZWB Cycling - Monday Ride
description:
Welcome to the weekly ZWB Cycling - Monday Ride! In the Zwift Racing League weeks we use this ride as a recon ride for the upcoming ZRL race. We ride the route together, point out the key segments and share tips & tricks. We aim to keep the pace steady but approachable. In weeks without a ZRL race we’ll pick a random route that still provides you with a good training session and a fun group ride.

ZWB Cycling is a Dutch cycling club and community. Thinking about joining us or looking for details? Visit www.zwbcycling.nl or leave us a message on Discord.

name: It's always a race "group ride" with DZR
description:
We set out to create a group ride, but let’s be honest: every group ride is really a race. So we’re making it official: this is a race. No categories. No separation. Everyone together. Sort it out on the road!

Choose your route: Long or Short. But don’t be fooled. Both will hurt! This isn’t a coffee ride.

But we’re nice people after all… we’ll throw you a bone: at least one route will be Route of the Week for bonus XP.

Rules: No PowerUps • No zPower • HRM required. Plain and simple, as it should be.

Find out more about Danish Zwift Racers at www.dzrracingseries.com

eventSubgroups.name: It's always a race "group ride" with DZR (A)
It's always a race "group ride" with DZR (B)

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - The Fan Flats
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: PACK🐺 Social + KOM After Party
description:
Join the PACK and experience a fun social group ride with an average lap pace of around 2w/kg. Everyone is welcome so come and enjoy the friendly supportive atmosphere with late join enabled if you miss the start. The Leader will always be at the correct pace, giving out instructions to help new riders as communication is the key to a good group ride.

After the event finishes, there is an optional fun After Party KOM challenge up the final climb. Ride with others or try to beat your HoloReplay.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: Norwegian Watts Club Group Ride
description:
Hey Zwifters!

Ready to roll with the coolest crew on the virtual roads? The Norwegian Watts Club is hosting a group ride and you're officially invited! Whether you're chasing watts or just cruising for good vibes, this is your hour to shine.

Everyone is welcome — no gatekeeping, no ego, just good company and smooth pedaling. Our ride leader will cruise at a friendly pace of 1.7–2.0 W/kg, with a little extra spice on the climbs (because gravity doesn’t care about your feelings 😅).

Stick close to the yellow beacon—that’s our golden glue keeping the group together. If you find yourself drifting off or facing technical gremlins, our trusty sweepers will swoop in like cycling superheroes to bring you back into the fold.

So bring your watts, your chat, and your best socks. Let’s make this ride one to remember!

See you on the start line 🚴‍♂️💨

name: Team CLS Racing - Pace 'n' Race: Recon and Activation
description:
A perfect activation ride ahead of Tuesday racing, or simply an enjoyable ride to start the week! 

This is ideal for racers participating in ZRL or DRS as this will often be a great recon opportunity on an upcoming route. Outside of these series, you will always have an enjoyable blast on a favorite route.

The ride will hold a steady pace to get the legs going and towards the end we will aim to form a PACE group that will remain steady, and a RACE group that will have the opportunity to get in a mini effort/race as a short blast to the finish!


Information and Rules
• Please follow the beacon's calls to ensure the group remains smooth and everyone gets the most out of the event.
• The Fence will be used and it is 'live' so if you go ahead of the group for too long, you will be removed from the event. 


WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social [Kit unlock]

TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426


TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: The HERD Kickstart
description:
The ride to kickstart your week! Our goal is to have fun, blow off some Monday steam and start our week like we want it to go on. Pace is 1.6 – 1.9 w/kg. We also select ride leader/courses that don't disadvantage heavier riders.

WE USE THE FENCE! The fence is in place to remind you to stay with the YELLOW BEACON.

This is a group ride and not a race, so please ride with the YELLOW BEACON. Stronger riders are encouraged to fall back and help the sweep team.

Join us on Discord during the ride to participate in audio conversation with one another. It will help the time pass by and give you a safe space to ask all the questions you have about Zwift, without feeling uncomfortable or risk being judged.

description:
Come join TNP in our monthly Individual Time Trial Series
We will be racing every Monday against the clock
GC will be decided on points not time, 1st place gets 1 point,
20th gets 20 etc - lowest total wins
Multiple Time slots available

name: Phoenix Rising From the Saddle
description:
From the Saddle is Phoenix Rising's highER paced ride. The leader will aim to stay between 1.9 and 2.2 w/kg, adjusting the speed slightly to keep the group together, especially up hills.
Occasionally this will be a chase ride on a crit-type course where the leader will keep pacing with the intent to get everyone to lap them.

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/mNMva8ekJR
Check us out on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/615972979730125/

name: GXY BLAST OFF! [1-1.4 WKG] CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY BLAST OFF!
Please join us as we set off on our weekly visit into the Zwift world at a beginners/recovery pace.
Blast off by name… but steady as we go, whilst we ride around predominantly flat or slightly undulating courses with
power and speed maintained to suite everyone on this GALAXY no drop ride.
A designated social discord channel is available to just chat or to learn from experienced leaders about how to get the
most out of your journey on the Zwift platform.
We ride as one under the guidance of an experienced leader and sweepers who are there to help and support you.
Their primary aim is of course to keep the group together whilst sharing knowledge and making the ride fun and
enjoyable for everyone.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed. We are looking forward to riding with you
on this, our launchpad ride.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link or by the email below:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY BLAST OFF! [1-1.4 WKG] CAT D (D)

name: WCC Wobble Group Workout
description:
Welcome to WCC’s Wobble Group Workout.

On this workout ride you will be completing this banded workout at your own FTP. Each workout will be lead by one of WCC’s great social ride leaders who will offer reminders to hydrate as well as encouragement throughout the 30-45 minute sessions.

All prompts for changed in power, cadence, ect will be tailored to your specific FTP on this banded ride without the concern of being dropped off the back of the group as long as you keep the cranks spinning.

We will be making use of Zwift’s Ari ours workout plans rotating through a variety of skills as we move throughout the different sessions within the plan..

If you are looking to join WCC we can be found at any of the following links…

Via Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/groups/2665592713734072/?ref=share

website
https://www.wobblecycleclub.com

Or via the Club on the ZC app
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/wcc/home

name: The Stampede Challenge by Herd Racing
description:
Join Herd Racing for our monthly TT challenge. For the month of January
it's 2 laps of Handful of Gravel. You can race this as many times as you'd
like and try to beat your time and your best time counts in the monthly
standings https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=3093

For these events drafting is turned off, so it's just you and the clock. Is
the gravel, the MTB or the TT bike fastest? Maybe even a road bike?!?!
Pick your fastest equipment and give it your best shot! Join us in the Herd
Racing League facebook or Discord server to chat pacing strategies, gear,
and more.
https://discord.gg/DrAXaCb7Tq

name: Sledgehammer Inbetweeners Races
description:
Welcome to the Sledgehammer series.
These races are designed to equalise the categories as much as possible and encourage team riding between riders of different strengths. Please enter your appropriate category according to your Zwiftpower race category.
All out of category entrants will be excluded from the Zwiftpower results.
Organiser's Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2388754431339748/
Race, steer, draft. Team up and chase those faster riders!

name: Ventouro Social Group Ride
description:
A fun morning ride to get the legs turning for the week. Enjoy being part of a group and riding to your ability. The ride is generally led by ex Pro Tour rider and double Olympian Tim Gudsell, or one of Ventouro’s great crew.

The ride starts steady and moderate pace most around the flat streets of London (2-2.5w/kg) no need to load up the watts more than 100w. There will be a fence on just after the start so please respect it. It comes off after 14km.
It starts climbing at KM 13. With 5km up hill and the last 2km being the steepest. Then a fast run down to the finish.
It will split on the hill, so you can go for gold to the finish, or keep it in zone and make your way up at a steady pace. Enjoy!

Ventouro is an adventure cycling business offering guided tours of the world's most beautiful destinations. From weekend retreats in New Zealand's wine regions, to week-long journeys through the fjords of Norway, The Great Wall of China, or the French Alps.

From a standing start in 2014 Ventouro has established itself as a globally recognised luxury-cycling business with an established high-net-worth client-base in America, China, Australia and New Zealand.

name: Team KST's "Le Rouleur" Race
description:
Team KST’s “Le Rouleur” Race

Team Koege SportsTerapi bring you this serie of weekly races. The “Le Rouleur serie” is four races every week. Why the name “Le Rouleur” ?
because a true Rouleur is a rider who is considered a good all-rounder.

So prepare yourself to be challenged in different terrains. Our courses have it all: Flat, hilly, mountain, downhill or sprint you name it…. you got it.
By winning the different races you will prove to yourself and your surroundings, that you are a true Rouleur.

These races requires the best of you.
We aim for a good and fair race and a great work out.


Important
Rules
- No TT Bikes
- Push Up allowed
- Choose right Cat.
- Enter right weight
The rest is pretty simpel… ride as hard as you can:)

“Gas Geben”

FOLLOW TEAM KST Fan page on FB page

https://www.facebook.com/Team-KST-powered-by-Køge-SportsTerapi-101151418716498/

name: ZTPL Cycling Club Coffee Ride
description:
☀️Weszliśmy w sezon wiosenno-letni.☀️
Część naszych eventów zostaje w kalendarzu, ale może zdarzyć się tak że nie będzie na nich naszych liderów jazd.
Jeśli tak się zdarzy, gorąca prośba do Was o jazdę wg. założeń danego eventu.
Nasze eventy i liderzy ZTPL Cycling Club wrócą ponownie na jesień 2026.
Póki co RIDE ON i korzystajcie z pogody, a jeśli nie możecie to bawcie się dobrze podczas naszych eventów.

Zapraszamy na wspólny event z ZTPL Cycling Club. Pamiętajcie o wyborze właściwej grupy.

Z założenia nasze jazdy prowadzą liderzy, którzy pomagają trzymać peleton w ryzach. Jeśli podczas eventu zabraknie lidera, starajcie się trzymać tempo, na które się umówiliśmy w momencie gdy dołączyliście do wybranej grupy. To od Was zależy jak będziecie się bawić 😊
Po ukończonym evencie, jeśli jechaliście z nami pierwszy raz, w Waszym wirtualnym garażu będzie czekała nasza teamowa koszulka!
◾️Grupa E jest grupą początkującą. Tempo jest spokojne, uczymy się jazdy w grupie, jest czas żeby porozmawiać. Jedziemy w przedziale 1.4-1.8 w/kg
◾️Grupa D przeznaczona jest dla średnio zaawansowanych. Tempo będzie oscylować w okolicach 1.8-2.2 w/kg z przodu peletonu (możliwe akcenty).
Ważne: Jeśli wasze FTP jest w granicach 2w/kg to nie będzie grupa dla Was.
◾️Grupa C jedzie w tempie 2.5-3 w/kg (możliwe akcenty). Jeśli Wasze FTP jest w granicy 2.5w/kg nie będzie to dla Was Coffee Ride :)

◾️ Jeśli chcecie przejechać jazdę w tempie Coffee Ride wybierzcie o jedną grupę niżej niż sugeruje wam wasze FTP lub zwiftpower :)
◾️ Wartości podane w opisach dotyczą średniego tempa z CAŁEJ jazdy (nie uwzględniają interwałów, które mogą wystąpić podczas np. podjazdów)
◾️ PAMIĘTAJCIE że podczas jazd grupowych NIE UŻYWAMY ROWERÓW CZASOWYCH!
◾️ Starajcie się pilnować peletonu i nie zgubić koła, samemu bardzo trudno jest odrobić stratę.
◾️ Proszę słuchajcie poleceń lidera, które podczas jazdy wyświetlane są na środku ekranu. Szczególnie jeśli lider zauważy że grupa się porwała.
◾️ Lider, który prowadzi grupę podświetlony jest na żółto.
◾️ Starajcie się jechać blisko lidera, starając się go nie wyprzedać i nie wyjeżdżać przed bramę, która informuje Was że jedziecie za szybko.

◾️Podoba się Wam to co robimy? Zawsze możecie postawić Waszym kochanym administratorom oraz liderom naszych jazd wirtualną kawę :) https://buycoffee.to/ztplcc
◾️Jeśli chcecie sprawić sobie już teraz naszą nową teamową koszulkę ZTPL Cycling Club zapraszamy bezpośrednio na stronę https://inoni.cc/?s=ztpl&post_type=product
◾️Cała gama naszych koszulek oraz informacje o kolejnych Pre-Orderach znajdziecie na https://ztpl.cc/stroje-ztpl-cc/

#RideOn #ZTPLCC

[ENG]
☀️We’re now in the spring and summer season.☀️
Some of our events will stay in the calendar, but there may be times when our ride leaders won’t be there. If that happens, we’d really appreciate it if you could still ride according to the plan of the event.
Our ZTPL Cycling Club events will be back in autumn 2026.
For now — RIDE ON, enjoy the good weather, and if you can’t, just have a great time at our events.

We would like to invite you to ZTPL Cycling Club Coffee Ride.
In order to ride with Coffee Ride pace, please choose your group wisely.
Our rides are usually led by leaders who help keep the peloton in check. If there is no leader during the event, try to stick to the pace we agreed on when you joined the selected group.
◾️Group E is a beginner group. The pace is calm, we learn how to ride in a Zwift peleton. We will ride with 1.4-1.8 w/kg pace
◾️Group D is between group E and more advanced Group C. Group pace 1.8-2.2w/kg.
Important: If your FTP is around 2 w/kg, it won’t be a Coffee Ride for you.
◾️Group C is more advanced with pace 2.5-3 w/kg

◾️Please listen to the leader’s commands which are displayed in yellow during the whole ride. The leader who leads the group is highlighted in yellow. Please don’t pass the leader.
◾️Please follow the leader instructions! Especially if the leader notices that the group has been swept away. The leader is highlighted in yellow.
◾️At the and wait for the leader’s command: GOGOGO and start your sprint. Enjoy!

◾If you would like to get yourself our ZTPL Cycling Club team jersey please visit our shop https://inoni.cc/en/?s=ztpl&post_type=product

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (L'equipe Provence)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by L'equipe Provence (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b4bafc65-a1f2-49a7-a4ab-6cb7326d34b0).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wmz #wtrl #zrl

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (L'equipe Provence) (E)

eventSeries.name: ZRL 25/26 R4
eventSeries.description: Zwift Racing League Season 20 (2025/26 - Round 4). Includes all races and official recon rides

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: USMeS – Back to Base [D] 1.8-2.2w/kg
description:
USMeS – Back to Base
Monday Recovery and Regroup, Ease back into the week with Back to Base. This is our Monday recovery ride. Designed to flush out the legs after your weekend missions. Our second recovery ride of the day, if you missed our Back to Work Ride earlier. This ride offering a smooth roll with just a touch of extra effort on the climbs to keep things lively.

 

✅ Pace: 1.8–2.2 w/kg average
✅ Style: Steady endurance with light pushes on the hills
✅ Fence: In use between efforts, we like to drop it for segments but regroup quickly

 

Ride Format:

Mostly steady Zone 2 riding
Gentle tempo on climbs—never all-out
Friendly chat encouraged
 

Expectations:
✅ Respect the recovery vibe - this isn’t a race
✅ When the fence comes down for a segment, regroup promptly behind the beacon
✅ Hydrate, spin easy, and enjoy the company

 

If you’ve logged big miles over the weekend, this is the perfect ride to reset, recover, and roll back to base, starting the week strong.

 

#conqueringlimits

#challengeaccepted

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter
